title: Eddy — fluid studio
description: A real-time, from-scratch incompressible Navier–Stokes AND magnetohydrodynamics studio in pure TypeScript. Stable Fluids with semi-Lagrangian + MacCormack advection and FOUR pressure-projection Poisson solvers over one identical Neumann/obstacle stencil — red-black SOR, matrix-free Jacobi-preconditioned Conjugate Gradients, a from-scratch geometric MULTIGRID V-cycle (work-optimal O(N), grid-independent convergence), and MULTIGRID-PRECONDITIONED CG (MGCG) — plus OPEN inflow/outflow boundaries that turn the tank into a true channel so a von Kármán wake leaves the domain instead of recirculating. NEW — a full MAGNETOHYDRODYNAMICS pillar: an in-plane magnetic field coupled to the flow through the LORENTZ FORCE (the divergence-free magnetic tension (B·∇)B, the magnetic pressure absorbed into the velocity's own projection) and the INDUCTION EQUATION (semi-Lagrangian transport + field-line stretching (B·∇)u + Ohmic resistivity), kept solenoidal (∇·B = 0) by the very same Hodge projection that keeps the flow incompressible — no monopoles, no new linear algebra. It carries the canonical plasma physics: ALFVÉN WAVES whose dispersion ω = v_A·k is timed live against the closed form, the ORSZAG–TANG vortex steepening into thin CURRENT SHEETS, magnetic RECONNECTION at a Harris current sheet, and field-line stretching (flux-freezing). Carries vorticity confinement, a Boussinesq temperature field driving real thermal convection, a reactive combustion model with variable-density buoyancy, RGB ink with its own Schmidt-number diffusivity, and arbitrary solid obstacles. A flagship LAGRANGIAN-COHERENT-STRUCTURE (FTLE) render mode integrates the flow map to expose the hidden transport skeleton. NEW — a SECOND, INDEPENDENT SOLVER built from the bottom up: a from-scratch D2Q9 LATTICE BOLTZMANN kinetic method (the KINETIC LAB) that never writes Navier–Stokes down at all — it streams and collides a nine-velocity particle distribution f, and the same incompressible fluid EMERGES, provably, through the Chapman–Enskog expansion (ν = c_s²(τ−½), measured back out of a decaying shear wave on the Verify page). It carries THREE collision operators — single-relaxation BGK, TWO-RELAXATION-TIME (TRT) whose magic parameter Λ=3/16 pins the bounce-back wall exactly so a body-forced channel reproduces the analytic POISEUILLE parabola to ~0.1% (where BGK slips), and MULTIPLE-RELAXATION-TIME (MRT) that relaxes all nine physical moments independently in moment space (M·f, inverse built numerically at load), damping the unphysical ghost modes for maximal stability; Guo forcing (projected into moment space for MRT); half-way bounce-back walls plus a moving-wall LID-DRIVEN CAVITY; a Zou–He inlet / extrapolation-outflow open channel; and a SMAGORINSKY LES sub-grid model whose strain rate is read locally from the non-equilibrium stress Π^neq (free at every node — no finite differences). Flow past a cylinder sheds a VON KÁRMÁN VORTEX STREET whose STROUHAL NUMBER the lab measures live against Williamson's experimental correlation, with drag/lift from a from-scratch momentum-exchange sum (lift oscillates at the shedding frequency, drag at twice it). NEWEST — a THIRD kinetic solver and a whole new physics: MULTIPHASE flow via the from-scratch SHAN–CHEN PSEUDOPOTENTIAL method (the PHASE LAB). One short-range attractive force between lattice sites — F = −G·ψ(x)·Σ wᵢψ(x+eᵢ)eᵢ with ψ(ρ)=1−e^(−ρ) — gives the fluid a non-ideal van-der-Waals equation of state, and below the EXACT critical strength G_c = −4 a uniform fluid spontaneously UNMIXES into liquid and vapour with a real SURFACE TENSION — no interface tracking, no level set. Droplets obey LAPLACE'S LAW Δp = σ/R (measured live and pinned across four radii on the Verify page), two drops COALESCE, a noisy fluid SPINODALLY DECOMPOSES into a coarsening foam, fluid–solid ADHESION sets a tunable CONTACT ANGLE (hydrophilic↔hydrophobic), and under gravity drops RAIN onto a wettable floor — while the antisymmetric internal force conserves momentum to round-off. NEWEST — a FOURTH kinetic solver: the MULTI-COMPONENT Shan–Chen model of TWO genuinely distinct, IMMISCIBLE fluids (oil/water style), each with its own D2Q9 distribution and coupled only by a short-range CROSS-REPULSION F_σ = −G·ρ_σ·Σ wᵢρ_σ′(x+eᵢ)eᵢ — so above a critical coupling a blended mixture DEMIXES into pure domains with an emergent interfacial SURFACE TENSION, and the Phase lab's two-fluid mode runs the RAYLEIGH–TAYLOR instability (a heavy fluid fingering down through a light one), RAYLEIGH–PLATEAU thread breakup (a liquid filament pinching into a row of drops), a suspended DROP reading Laplace's law live, spinodal DEMIXING, and a sessile drop whose CONTACT ANGLE bends with the wall's wettability — both species' masses and the total momentum conserved to round-off via a shared momentum-conserving common velocity. NEWEST — a FIFTH kinetic model and a whole new physics: THERMAL convection via a from-scratch DOUBLE-DISTRIBUTION thermal lattice Boltzmann (the CONVECTION LAB). A SECOND D2Q9 distribution g rides the same lattice and carries the temperature as an advected–diffused scalar (T = Σ gᵢ, with a thermal diffusivity α = c_s²(τ_g−½) fixed only by its relaxation time — the exact scalar twin of the Chapman–Enskog viscosity law), two-way coupled to the flow through a per-node BOUSSINESQ BUOYANCY force (hot fluid is lighter, so it rises). From nothing but stream + collide on two lattices, thermal convection EMERGES — and it is quantitatively right: RAYLEIGH–BÉNARD convection rolls switch on at the textbook CRITICAL RAYLEIGH NUMBER Ra_c ≈ 1708 (measured live by a linear-growth-rate bisection on the Verify page), the differentially-heated DE VAHL DAVIS cavity reproduces the tabulated NUSSELT NUMBER to a few percent, and a continuous THERMAL PLUME mushrooms off a hot floor patch — with fixed-temperature (anti-bounce-back Dirichlet) and adiabatic (bounce-back zero-flux) thermal walls, the NUSSELT NUMBER read live as the convective heat-transport boost, and the conduction limit pinned to an exact linear profile (Nu = 1). NEWEST — the studio's first COMPRESSIBLE solver, and with it a whole branch of fluid dynamics the five incompressible solvers above cannot touch: a from-scratch finite-volume GODUNOV scheme for the compressible EULER equations (the GAS LAB) that captures genuine SHOCK WAVES and contact discontinuities. It marches the conserved variables (ρ, ρu, ρv, E) with a minmod-limited MUSCL-HANCOCK reconstruction — second order in space AND time (a half-step Hancock predictor evolves each face state by its own flux), with a positivity safeguard that drops a cell to first order before a limited slope could reconstruct negative density or pressure — and an HLLC three-wave approximate Riemann flux (the contact-and-shear-resolving Godunov flux, where plain HLL/Rusanov would smear them), Strang dimensionally split in 2-D and capped by the CFL signal speed |u|+a, over transmissive / reflective / periodic boundaries with an optional gravity source. It ships an EXACT RIEMANN SOLVER — a Newton solve of the pressure function f_L(p)+f_R(p)+Δu=0 plus a self-similar sampler — wired both as a live pale-line overlay on the SOD and LAX shock tubes (watch the second-order scheme sit down on the analytic answer) and as the verification ground truth: the Sod star state comes out p*=0.30313, u*=0.92745 to five figures, and the finite-volume Sod tube CONVERGES to the exact profile in L1. Eight scenes — the Sod and Lax tubes, a SEDOV point blast, the four-shock 2-D RIEMANN problem, a compressible KELVIN–HELMHOLTZ roll-up, RAYLEIGH–TAYLOR under gravity, the LISKA–WENDROFF implosion and a Mach-1.5 SHOCK striking a light gas BUBBLE (Richtmyer–Meshkov) — render through density / pressure / speed / Mach / SCHLIEREN views (a synthetic shadowgraph of |∇ρ| that reads shock fronts like a wind-tunnel knife-edge photograph). Twenty-eight scenes — the von Kármán vortex street (closed AND open-channel), Rayleigh–Bénard convection, a buoyant plume, Kelvin–Helmholtz shear, the lid-driven cavity, a self-sustaining Fire, the Taylor–Green vortex, decaying AND continuously-forced 2-D turbulence, the double-shear-layer benchmark, plus the Orszag–Tang vortex, magnetic reconnection, an Alfvén wave and a magnetized shear layer — plus a live SPECTRA LAB running a from-scratch 2-D FFT to plot the kinetic-energy spectrum E(k), its k^-3/k^-5/3 cascade slopes, AND the spectral energy flux Π(k). Dye/speed/vorticity/pressure/temperature/LCS plus |B|/current-jz/B-field-line views, animated Line Integral Convolution, schlieren and Q-criterion visualisation, streamline + tracer overlays, a live hover probe, WebM capture, and an 82-check numerical verification suite (incl. grid-independent multigrid, MGCG, analytic diffusion-decay and FTLE strain rates, FFT/Parseval identities, exact energy-transfer conservation, the open-channel through-flow, the MHD claims: ∇·B cleaning, the Alfvén dispersion relation, ideal-MHD energy conservation, flux-freezing and the Orszag–Tang current-sheet benchmark, AND the Lattice-Boltzmann claims: exact equilibrium moments, mass conservation, the Chapman–Enskog viscosity ν=c_s²(τ−½) from a shear wave, the analytic Poiseuille parabola from the TRT magic wall, and the strain rate read from Π^neq, AND the multiphase claims: the exact G_c=−4 critical point, mass conservation, flat-interface mechanical equilibrium, Laplace's law Δp=σ/R across four droplet radii, and internal-force momentum conservation, AND the multi-component two-fluid claims: spontaneous demixing above the critical coupling, per-species mass conservation, inter-species momentum conservation, and Laplace's law for a drop of one fluid suspended in the other, AND the thermal-LBM claims: the scalar Chapman–Enskog diffusivity α=c_s²(τ_g−½) from a decaying temperature wave, the exact conduction limit (a linear profile and Nusselt number Nu=1), adiabatic heat conservation, the critical Rayleigh number Ra_c≈1708 for the onset of convection, and the de Vahl Davis cavity Nusselt number, AND the compressible-Euler claims: the exact Sod star state p*=0.30313/u*=0.92745, the Riemann sampler's iso-pressure/iso-velocity contact structure, HLLC flux consistency F(U,U)=F(U), the captured Sod shock satisfying all three RANKINE–HUGONIOT jump conditions to machine precision, L1 convergence of the finite-volume Sod tube to the exact Riemann solution, round-off mass/momentum/energy conservation under periodic boundaries, strong-blast positivity, and exact uniform advection) that proves the solver's claims live in the browser.
